name: Eddie Costa
description: American jazz musician; pianist and vibraphonist (1930–1962)
abstract: Edwin James Costa (August 14, 1930 – July 28, 1962) was an American jazz pianist,  vibraphonist, composer and arranger. In 1957, he was chosen as DownBeat jazz critics' new star on piano and vibes – the first time that one artist won two categories in the same year.

concerts.label: 20th Century Innovations: Early Experiments with Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: Manny Albam
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Costa, an incredible piano player... I knew he was going to be there... Eddie Costa suggested Vinny Burke. They had been working as a duo for a while.
about_quotes.doc_title: Manny Albam interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: There was a guy on the scene that you probably maybe have not heard his name. Eddie Costa? ... I remember Joe Morello loved Eddie Costa’s playing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Toshiko Akiyoshi
about_quotes.evidence: Even those not that noted, Eddie Costa. There was a drum player, later he was playing with Eddie’s group or somebody like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toshiko Akiyoshi interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 17, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Eddie Costa -- I think he was vibes. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. These men were put all together in New York.

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: One of them was with John Costa – – Eddie Costa, a pianist from Canada. I have done three of those things
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
